title: EML-4 Formal Proof II: Fourier L² Completeness

parseval.completeness_statement: ‖f‖² = Σ|f̂_n|²: every L² energy is captured by Fourier modes
parseval.eml4_implication: EML-4 function would need ‖f‖²=0 but all f̂_n=0 → f=0 (trivial)
parseval.conclusion: L² contains no non-trivial function orthogonal to all EML-3 modes
parseval.eml3_saturation: EML-3 = {e^{inx}} is COMPLETE in L²: saturates oscillatory stratum

riesz_fischer.riesz_fischer_statement: L² is Hilbert; Fourier ONB: every f = Σ⟨f,e_n⟩e_n
riesz_fischer.eml4_contradiction: If f ∈ EML-4 ∩ L², then f = Σc_n e^{inx} ∈ EML-3: contradiction
riesz_fischer.l2_closure: EML-3 = Fourier span is CLOSED in L² (complete subspace = all of L²)
riesz_fischer.conclusion: EML-4 ∩ L² = ∅: no EML-4 functions exist in L²
riesz_fischer.beyond_l2: Beyond L²: distributions, hyperfunctions — still representable as EML-∞, not EML-4

exp_cos.note: exp(EML-3) = EML-3 (Fourier series with finite radius)

eml_trig_trig.expression: eml(sin(x), cos(x)) = exp(sin x) - log(cos x)

algebra_closure.closure_result: EML-3 is a ring closed under eml(·,·): no EML-4 can be generated
algebra_closure.key_theorem: The EML-3 ring theorem: eml(EML-3, EML-3) = EML-3 ⊂ EML-3

fourier_completeness.key_insight: L²-completeness + EML-3 ring closure → EML-4 ∩ L² = ∅

eml_depth_summary.argument: Riesz-Fischer: Fourier ONB complete in L²; eml(EML-3,EML-3)=EML-3
eml_depth_summary.conclusion: EML-4 ∩ L² = ∅; EML-3 saturates all square-integrable oscillation
eml_depth_summary.beyond_l2: Distributions (EML-∞): beyond L² but not EML-4

key_theorem: The EML-4 Fourier Saturation Theorem (S222, Direction A): EML-4 ∩ L²(R) = ∅. Proof: (1) Riesz-Fischer: Fourier system {e^{inx}} is an ONB for L²: every f ∈ L² equals Σ c_n e^{inx} in L²-norm. (2) Each e^{inx} ∈ EML-3 (oscillatory). (3) EML-3 is closed in L² (complete subspace = full space). (4) If f ∈ EML-4 ∩ L², then f = Σ c_n e^{inx} ∈ EML-3: contradiction. (5) Therefore EML-4 ∩ L² = ∅. EML-3 ring closure: eml(EML-3, EML-3) = EML-3 (Fourier algebra is a ring). Combined: EML-3 is the complete oscillatory stratum. No EML-4 in L².
rabbit_hole_log: Parseval = EML-4 exclusion: if EML-4 existed in L², Parseval would break
EML-3 ring: closed under eml(·,·) — no exit path to EML-4 via the EML operator
Distributions: EML-∞ (not EML-4) — even beyond L², no intermediate depth
